description:
Ratspeak dashboard — app-owned IPC commands.

Tauri v2's capability system enforces permissions on *plugin* commands; app-owned `#[tauri::command]` fns registered via `generate_handler!` are implicitly callable from any window the app itself loads. Because the Ratspeak WebView loads only our own frontend from `tauri://localhost/index.html` (no remote origins, no iframes — see the `main` window + CSP in tauri.conf.json), the security boundary is 'our code calling our code,' which is the app's compile-time decision about what to expose.

Destructive commands (factory_reset, delete_identity, reset_database, identity_reset, clear_*) are reviewed for confirmation flow and surface a user-confirmation modal in the frontend (see dialogs.js::rsConfirm) before invocation. A future refactor could move the destructive subset to a plugin to gate it with a distinct permission.
